title: Implement backpressure responses and connection lifecycle state machine
purpose: Deliver the backpressure response vocabulary (Accept, Defer, Reject) per ADR-004 and the connection lifecycle state machine (connecting, connected, reconnecting, disconnected) per ADR-008. Backpressure is surfaced as typed responses on every publish operation -- the SDK never silently consumes pressure signals (constraint CN6). The connection state machine provides the foundation for automatic reconnection with exponential backoff and jitter (constraint CN7), and subscription state recovery from the last acknowledged sequence.
task: Create pressure.rs for backpressure types, and the connection/ module with mod.rs, lifecycle.rs, and recovery.rs. PressureResponse is an enum returned from publish operations. The connection state machine is a typed state enum with defined transitions. Recovery tracks per-subscription sequence numbers for replay on reconnect. IN SCOPE: backpressure types, connection state machine, reconnection strategy, subscription recovery. OUT OF SCOPE: connection pooling (SDK-004), concrete transport (SDK-004), channel/conversation logic (SDK-002).

requirements.title: Define PressureResponse enum with Accept, Defer, and Reject variants
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define a PressureResponse enum with three variants: Accept (message delivered), Defer { delay: Duration } (message buffered, estimated delay provided), and Reject { reason: String } (consumer overwhelmed, message shed). WHEN a publish operation completes, THE SYSTEM SHALL return a PressureResponse alongside the result. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT silently convert Defer or Reject to Accept.
acceptance: PressureResponse enum exists with exactly three variants: Accept, Defer, Reject
Defer carries a Duration field indicating estimated delay
Reject carries a reason String describing why the message was shed
PressureResponse derives Debug, Clone, and PartialEq
PressureResponse is re-exported from the crate root

requirements.title: Implement connection lifecycle state machine with observable transitions
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define a ConnectionState enum with four states: Connecting, Connected, Reconnecting { attempt: u32 }, and Disconnected { reason: DisconnectReason }. WHEN a state transition occurs, THE SYSTEM SHALL emit a ConnectionEvent to all registered observers. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT allow invalid transitions (e.g. Disconnected to Connected without passing through Connecting). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT expose the state machine's internal transition logic to callers.
acceptance: ConnectionState enum has exactly four variants: Connecting, Connected, Reconnecting, Disconnected
Reconnecting carries an attempt counter
Disconnected carries a DisconnectReason enum (Normal, Error, Timeout)
A ConnectionEvents stream is available for observing state transitions
Transition from Disconnected directly to Connected is rejected at compile time or returns an error

requirements.title: Implement exponential backoff with jitter for reconnection
requirements.spec: WHEN the connection transitions to Reconnecting, THE SYSTEM SHALL compute the next retry delay as min(base_delay * 2^attempt, max_delay) plus a random jitter factor. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT use fixed-interval retry (constraint CN7). THE SYSTEM SHALL accept configurable base_delay and max_delay parameters. THE SYSTEM SHALL reset the attempt counter to zero when a connection is successfully established.
acceptance: Reconnection delay for attempt N equals min(base_delay * 2^N, max_delay) + jitter
Jitter is a random value between 0 and 50% of the computed delay
base_delay and max_delay are configurable via a ReconnectConfig struct
After successful connection, the next reconnection attempt starts at attempt 0
No code path uses a fixed retry interval

requirements.title: Implement subscription state recovery from last acknowledged sequence
requirements.spec: WHEN the connection transitions from Reconnecting to Connected, THE SYSTEM SHALL replay each active subscription from its last acknowledged sequence number. THE SYSTEM SHALL track per-subscription sequence numbers as messages are acknowledged. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT request full replay from sequence zero if a prior acknowledged position exists.
acceptance: SubscriptionRecovery struct tracks a map of subscription_id to last_acknowledged_sequence
On reconnect, each subscription's resume request includes its last acknowledged sequence
A subscription with no prior acknowledgement starts from sequence zero
A subscription with acknowledged sequence N resumes from sequence N+1
Recovery state is cleared when a subscription is explicitly unsubscribed

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement connection pooling (deferred to SDK-004).
SHALL NOT implement concrete wire protocol transport.
SHALL NOT implement channel or conversation logic.
SHALL NOT silently consume, buffer, or retry on backpressure signals.
SHALL NOT use fixed-interval retry for reconnection.
verification: cargo clippy -p agora-sdk -- -D warnings passes with no diagnostics
cargo fmt -p agora-sdk --check reports no formatting issues
Unit tests cover all four connection state transitions
Unit tests verify exponential backoff delay computation for attempts 0 through 10
Unit tests verify subscription recovery sequence tracking
No file exceeds 500 lines of code
